description: The ruling nobility and their military apparatus. They offer legitimate, well-paying work — and do not forgive slights.
hostile_action_description: Knights or royal soldiers are dispatched to make an example of the guild.

description: A powerful religious institution with political reach. They deal in monsters, cults, and lost relics. Angering them closes doors.
hostile_action_description: Inquisitors investigate the guild and deny access to Church-affiliated healers and supplies.

description: A coalition of traders and financiers. They pay well and complain loudly. Reliable clients — unless crossed.
hostile_action_description: Assassins are hired through underworld contacts; future contracts mysteriously dry up.

description: Criminals, thieves, and worse. Their work is off the books and pays in coin that asks no questions.
hostile_action_description: Sabotage events begin; night raids on the guild by armed brigands.

description: Farmers, labourers, and townsfolk. They can't pay much, but there are many of them, and reputation spreads fast among the common people.
hostile_action_description: Reputation damage only. No organised force, but local cooperation dries up.
